title: Selling Premium Mountain Air
tagline: Episode 1: Why breathe free when you can breathe FANCY?
scene_prompt: A very serious businessman in an expensive suit on top of a snowy mountain, carefully bottling fresh air into mason jars labeled 'LUXURY ARTISAN MOUNTAIN AIR - $99 per breath', with a cash register and a long line of gullible customers waiting. Cartoon style, vibrant colors, comedic.
narration: Why work a regular job when you can just stand on a mountain and bottle the air? Step one: climb mountain. Step two: open jar. Step three: close jar very quickly. Step four: sell jar for ninety-nine dollars and describe the 'terroir.' Totally illegal. Totally worth it.
music_prompt: Dramatic orchestral heist music that suddenly becomes a cheerful salesman jingle, comedic and upbeat

title: The Neighborhood Toll Booth Scheme
scene_prompt: A homeowner in a fluorescent vest standing in their own driveway next to a homemade wooden toll booth with a striped barrier arm, collecting coins from confused neighbors in cars. A hand-painted sign says 'TOLL: $2 per car. EXACT CHANGE ONLY.' Cartoon style, funny.
narration: Did you know that your driveway is YOUR land? Technically. Probably. So I installed a toll booth. Now every car that drives past owes me two dollars. I've made forty-seven cents in three weeks. The neighbors are furious. The HOA has sent fourteen letters. I've never been happier.
music_prompt: Triumphant medieval fanfare that plays every time a car pays the toll, absurdly dramatic

title: Charging Cats Rent
scene_prompt: An overly formal landlord in a tiny suit presenting a rental agreement to a very unimpressed cat sitting on a couch. The lease document has a paw print signature line. The cat is looking the other way. A stack of overdue rent notices sits on the table. Cartoon style, cute and funny.
narration: Did you know the neighborhood cats have been using my porch rent-free for three years? That ends today. I've drafted legally binding lease agreements. The cats have been informed. Their response was to knock my coffee off the table. I'm taking them to small claims court.
music_prompt: Fancy jazz landlord music that slowly falls apart into chaotic cat meowing sounds

title: The Pyramid Scheme But Actual Pyramids
scene_prompt: An enthusiastic salesman in a sparkly blazer standing in front of a large whiteboard showing a diagram of tiny pyramids stacked in a pyramid shape. He's selling miniature decorative pyramids to a confused audience. The pyramids are very poorly made out of cardboard. Cartoon style.
narration: My pyramid scheme is completely different from other pyramid schemes because mine involves ACTUAL pyramids. You buy a tiny pyramid. Then you sell pyramids to your friends. They sell pyramids to their friends. Nobody knows why. Eventually everyone owns a pyramid. The Egyptians can't sue me.
music_prompt: Ancient Egyptian flute music that becomes an upbeat MLM recruitment anthem

title: Selling Homework to Birds
tagline: Episode 5: The avian tutoring market is UNTAPPED.
scene_prompt: A kid at a desk selling completed math homework sheets to pigeons who are lined up with tiny coin purses. The pigeons look very studious and concerned about their grades. A sign says 'HOMEWORK 4 SALE - 3 seeds OR 50 cents.' Cartoon style, absurd and funny.
narration: Birds are incredibly smart. Crows can recognize faces. Ravens solve puzzles. So why are they not in school? I've been selling completed homework to the local pigeon flock for six months. They mostly eat it. But Mr. Flaps got a B-plus on his algebra quiz and I take full credit.
music_prompt: School bell music mixed with bird chirping and the sound of pencils scratching paper, playfully academic

title: Counterfeiting Monopoly Money for Real
tagline: Episode 6: It IS money. I checked. Sort of.
scene_prompt: A very confident person at a McDonald's counter trying to pay with bright orange Monopoly money while the cashier stares in disbelief. The person is pointing at a printed legal document titled 'WHY THIS IS FINE.' Other customers in line are equally baffled. Cartoon style.
narration: Fun fact: Monopoly money is still printed on paper. Paper is used to make real money. Therefore, Monopoly money IS real money. I've tested this theory at twelve different fast food restaurants. The results have been mixed. I now own four parking tickets and a lifetime ban from Subway.
music_prompt: Jazzy heist music that ends with a sad trombone every time the cashier rejects the Monopoly money

title: Robin Hood But You Steal From Rich AND Give to Rich
scene_prompt: A person dressed as Robin Hood sneaking wallets from both rich people AND poor people, then giving them all to the same wealthy-looking man in a top hat who is laughing. Robin Hood looks very confused about his own plan. Cartoon style, satirical.
narration: I misread the Robin Hood story for twenty-three years. Apparently 'steal from the rich, give to the poor' does not mean steal from everyone and give it all to one rich guy. But I've already committed to the branding. The tights are non-refundable.
music_prompt: Medieval merry folk music that gradually becomes a confused and uncertain melody

title: Hacking by Buying All the Computers
tagline: Episode 8: If I own all computers, I own cyberspace.
scene_prompt: A determined teenager surrounded by thousands of laptops stacked to the ceiling, typing furiously on all of them simultaneously with multiple sets of arms, while screens show random green code and the words 'I AM IN.' The kid looks very proud. Cartoon style.
narration: My cybersecurity plan is simple. If I own ALL the computers, then I cannot be hacked. Because there are no other computers. I've spent my entire savings on laptops. I now own one thousand three hundred and forty-seven laptops. The internet is still working. I don't understand why.
music_prompt: 90s hacker movie synth music with dial-up modem sounds and keyboard typing sfx

title: Forging Art So Bad It Becomes Valuable
scene_prompt: An amateur painter presenting terrible stick-figure forgeries of famous masterpieces at a fancy auction house. Art critics in berets are examining the terrible paintings through monocles and stroking their chins thoughtfully. The auction bidding paddle is raised to $50,000. Cartoon style.
narration: I tried to forge the Mona Lisa. It looked like a potato with hair. I tried to forge The Starry Night. It looked like someone sneezed on a dark canvas. But apparently, contemporary art critics think I'm making a powerful statement about the deconstruction of classical forms. My potato lady sold for fifty thousand dollars.
music_prompt: Pretentious classical piano that becomes increasingly confused and avant-garde

title: Smuggling Sandwiches Past Airport Security
tagline: Episode 10: No one suspects the man with forty-seven sandwiches.
scene_prompt: A person in an enormous trench coat at airport security with sandwiches literally falling out everywhere - from the sleeves, the collar, the pockets. TSA agents look overwhelmed. The person is maintaining eye contact and sweating profusely. Cartoon style, chaotic and funny.
narration: Airport sandwiches cost twelve dollars. My sandwiches cost me three dollars at home. I am not paying twelve dollars for a sandwich. I am also not going to check my sandwiches. So I wore my sandwich coat. I thought forty-seven sandwiches was a reasonable number. The TSA did not agree.
music_prompt: Mission impossible theme but every time a sandwich falls out it plays a cartoon boing sound
